title: W7 B Partial Derivatives
body:
Mathematical Economics

Juan Carlos Carbajal

School of Economics UNSWвҖ“Sydney

Week 7B вҖ” Term 1, 2026

1/15  Partial Derivatives

2/15   Examples

Example 1

Let S = Rn ++ and f : S вҶ’ R be defined by

f (x) = xОұ 1 Оұ2 Оұn 1 x2 В· В· В· xn .

The derivative of f at x вҲҲ S is the linear map Df (x) represented by the gradient vector вҲҮf (x) вҲҲ Rn , where   вҲҮf (x) = Оұ 1 x1 f (x), Оұ x2 f (x), . . . , Оұ xn n f (x) .

It would be hard to verify that this is the derivative of a Cobb-Douglas function using definitions.

3/15   Partial Derivatives

Instead of computing Df (x) from definition, suppose we distort xi while keeping xвҲ’i fixed. This leads to the notion of partial derivatives.

Definition 1

Let S вҠҶ Rn be open and f : S вҶ’ R. The i-th partial derivative of f at x is defined by f (x1 , . . . , xiвҲ’1 , xi + t, xi+1 , . . . , xn ) вҲ’ f (x1 , . . . , xn ) lim , tвҶ’0 t

provided this limit exists.

4/15   Partial Derivatives

Instead of computing Df (x) from definition, suppose we distort xi while keeping xвҲ’i fixed. This leads to the notion of partial derivatives.

Definition 1

Let S вҠҶ Rn be open and f : S вҶ’ R. The i-th partial derivative of f at x is defined by f (x1 , . . . , xiвҲ’1 , xi + t, xi+1 , . . . , xn ) вҲ’ f (x1 , . . . , xn ) lim , tвҶ’0 t

provided this limit exists.

Write (x1 , . . . , xiвҲ’1 , xi + t, xi+1 , . . . , xn ) as x + t ei . The i-th partial derivative of f at x is then вҲӮ f (x) f (x + t ei ) вҲ’ f (x) = lim . вҲӮ xi tвҶ’0 t

4/15   Example

Example 2

Let f : R2 вҶ’ R be defined by f (x1 , x2 ) = x21 x2 + x2 .

Partial derivative with respect to x1 : fix x2 and compute вҲӮf (x) f (x1 + t, x2 ) вҲ’ f (x1 , x2 ) вҲӮx1 tвҶ’0 t [(x1 + t)2 x2 + x2 ] вҲ’ [x21 x2 + x2 ] tвҶ’0 t [(x1 + t)2 вҲ’ x21 ] x2 tвҶ’0 t (2x1 t + t2 ) x2 tвҶ’0 t h i = lim 2x1 x2 + t x2 = 2x1 x2 . tвҶ’0

5/15   Partial Derivatives vs the Derivative

The relationship between partial derivatives and the derivative Df is more complicated than what appears to be.

Intuitively, the partial derivative restricts the way we approach x. в—Ұ Instead of u вҶ’ x, we move only in the i-th dimension.

6/15   Partial Derivatives vs the Derivative

The relationship between partial derivatives and the derivative Df is more complicated than what appears to be.

Intuitively, the partial derivative restricts the way we approach x. в—Ұ Instead of u вҶ’ x, we move only in the i-th dimension.

One can show that if f : S вҶ’ R is differentiable at x вҲҲ U , then the i-th partial derivative must exist, for i = 1, . . . , n. Moreover,   вҲӮ f (x) вҲӮ f (x) Df (x)вҠӨ = вҲҮf (x) = , ... , вҲҲ Rn . вҲӮ x1 вҲӮ xn

But the converse is not true!

Knowing that each i-th partial derivative exists at x is not enough to assert the existence of the linear approximation Df (x).

6/15   Partial Derivatives vs the Derivative

Example 3

Let f (x1 , x2 ) be defined by пЈұ пЈІ x21 x22 , (x1 , x2 ) Мё= (0, 0) x1 + x2 f (x1 , x2 ) = пЈі0, (x1 , x2 ) = (0, 0).

One can show that both partial derivatives exist at (0, 0) but f is not differentiable at (0, 0).

в—Ұ Intuitively, if x2 = 0 then f (x1 , 0) is constant.

в—Ұ But f is not continuous at (0, 0), hence it cannot admit a derivative.

7/15   Existence of Derivative

Proposition 1

Let S вҠҶ Rn be open and f : S вҶ’ R. If all partial derivatives of f exist in an open neighbourhood of x вҲҲ S and are continuous at x, then f is differentiable at x. Moreover, вҲӮ f (x) Df (x) ei = , for all i = 1, . . . , n. вҲӮ xi

8/15   Existence of Derivative

Proposition 1

Let S вҠҶ Rn be open and f : S вҶ’ R. If all partial derivatives of f exist in an open neighbourhood of x вҲҲ S and are continuous at x, then f is differentiable at x. Moreover, вҲӮ f (x) Df (x) ei = , for all i = 1, . . . , n. вҲӮ xi

Exercise 1

For f (x) = xОұ Оұn 1 В· В· В· xn , show that at xМӮ > 0

вҲӮ f (xМӮ) Оұi = f (xМӮ). вҲӮ xi xМӮi

Conclude that f is differentiable everywhere on Rn ++ .

8/15  Directional Derivatives

9/15   Directional Derivatives

The j-th partial derivative of f at xМӮ specifies approaching xМӮ вҲҲ Rn in the direction ej = (0, . . . , 0, 1, 0, . . . , 0). в—Ұ This is why we write xМӮ + t ej for t вҲҲ R.

Consider any directional vector u such that вҲҘuвҲҘ = 1.

10/15   Directional Derivatives

The j-th partial derivative of f at xМӮ specifies approaching xМӮ вҲҲ Rn in the direction ej = (0, . . . , 0, 1, 0, . . . , 0). в—Ұ This is why we write xМӮ + t ej for t вҲҲ R.

Consider any directional vector u such that вҲҘuвҲҘ = 1. в—Ұ Approaching xМӮ in this direction obtains

f (xМӮ + t u) вҲ’ f (xМӮ) lim . tвҶ’0 t

в—Ұ When exists, this limit is called the directional derivative of f at xМӮ in the direction u.

10/15   Directional Derivatives vs the Derivative

Proposition 2

Let f be a real-valued function defined on the open set U вҠҶ Rn . If f is differentiable at xМӮ вҲҲ U and u is any directional vector in Rn , then the directional derivative of f at xМӮ in the direction u exists and equals Df (xМӮ) u.

Proof Omitted

11/15   Directional Derivatives vs the Derivative

Proposition 2

Let f be a real-valued function defined on the open set U вҠҶ Rn . If f is differentiable at xМӮ вҲҲ U and u is any directional vector in Rn , then the directional derivative of f at xМӮ in the direction u exists and equals Df (xМӮ) u.

Proof Omitted

Note of course that for all j = 1, . . . , n, вҲӮ f (xМӮ) = Df (xМӮ) ej . вҲӮ xj

11/15   Example

Example 4

Let f : R2 вҶ’ R be defined by f (x1 , x2 ) = x21 x2 + x2 .

Partial derivatives:

вҲӮf (x) вҲӮf (x) = 2x1 x2 and = x21 + 1. вҲӮx1 вҲӮx2

12/15   Example

Example 4

Let f : R2 вҶ’ R be defined by f (x1 , x2 ) = x21 x2 + x2 .

Partial derivatives:

вҲӮf (x) вҲӮf (x) = 2x1 x2 and = x21 + 1. вҲӮx1 вҲӮx2

For any u = (u1 , u2 ), the directional derivative is вҠӨ Df (x) u = 2x1 x2 , x21 + 1 (u1 , u2 )

= 2x1 x2 u1 + (x21 + 1)u2 .

12/15  Higher Order Derivatives

13/15   Second Order Derivatives

When f : S вҶ’ R is differentiable, for every x вҲҲ S we can find and associated вҲҮf (x) = Df (x)вҠӨ in Rn .

We can think of Df : S вҶ’ Rn as a function in its own right. в—Ұ If Df is continuous everywhere, we say that f is continuously differentiable.

в—Ұ We can ask if Df is differentiable at xМӮ.

в—Ұ When Df is differentiable at xМӮ, its derivative D [ Df (xМӮ) ] is

пЈ® вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) пЈ№ вҲӮx1 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx2 В·В·В· вҲӮx1 вҲӮxn пЈҜ пЈә пЈҜ 2 вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) пЈә пЈә пЈҜ вҲӮ f (xМӮ) В·В·В· пЈҜ вҲӮx2 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx2 вҲӮx2 вҲӮx2 вҲӮxn пЈә D2 f (xМӮ) = D [ Df (xМӮ) ] = пЈҜ пЈҜ . пЈә пЈҜ . .. .. .. пЈә пЈҜ . . . . пЈә пЈә пЈ° пЈ» 2 2 вҲӮ f (xМӮ) вҲӮ f (xМӮ) вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) вҲӮxn вҲӮx1 вҲӮxn вҲӮx2 В·В·В· вҲӮxn вҲӮxn

14/15   Higher Order Derivatives

The matrix D2 f (xМӮ) is called the Hessian matrix of f at xМӮ: пЈ® вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) пЈ№ вҲӮx1 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx2 В·В·В· вҲӮx1 вҲӮxn пЈҜ пЈә пЈҜ 2 вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) пЈә пЈә пЈҜ вҲӮ f (xМӮ) В·В·В· пЈҜ вҲӮx2 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx2 вҲӮx2 вҲӮx2 вҲӮxn пЈә D f (xМӮ) = пЈҜ пЈҜ . пЈә пЈҜ . .. .. .. пЈә пЈҜ . . . . пЈә пЈә пЈ° пЈ» вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) вҲӮxn вҲӮx1 вҲӮxn вҲӮx2 В·В·В· вҲӮxn вҲӮxn

When the function f is smooth (i.e., Df (x) is itself continuously differentiable), one can show that this matrix is symmetric:

вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) вҲӮ 2 f (xМӮ) = , for all i, j = 1, . . . , n. вҲӮxi вҲӮxj вҲӮxj вҲӮxi
